title: Sindhu Stuns World No. 5 Han Ye, Enters Japan Open Quarterfinals
indian_angle: India's top female shuttler PV Sindhu secured a significant victory over a higher-ranked opponent.
summary: PV Sindhu defeated world No. 5 Han Ye in straight games (21-16, 21-14) in just 35 minutes to advance to the quarterfinals of the Japan Open 2026.

title: World Junior Championships 2026 Selection Criteria Updated
indian_angle: The BAI has released updated eligibility and selection criteria for Indian players aiming for the BWF World Junior Championships 2026 in Cairo.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has issued a circular detailing the player eligibility and selection process for the BWF World Junior Championships 2026 to be held in Cairo, Egypt.

title: Delhi World Championships 2026: Badminton Needs Gen Z, Gen Alpha Watchers
indian_angle: With the BWF World Championships 2026 set to be held in Delhi, there's a focus on attracting younger Indian audiences to the sport.
summary: A discussion on the need for badminton to engage younger generations, Gen Z and Gen Alpha, especially with the BWF World Championships 2026 scheduled to take place in Delhi.

title: BAI Prez Sarma Addresses 'Monkeys at India Open' Incident
indian_angle: BAI President Himanta Biswa Sarma commented on an unusual incident involving monkeys during the India Open, highlighting domestic tournament challenges.
summary: BAI President Himanta Biswa Sarma responded to a query about monkeys interrupting play at the India Open, stating 'What is the problem if monkeys come to watch a game'.

title: 3x15 Scoring System Implemented for Domestic Junior Tournaments
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India has implemented a new 3x15 scoring system for U-13, U-15, and U-17 domestic ranking tournaments and National Championships.
summary: The 3x15 scoring system will be implemented in all domestic ranking tournaments and National Championships for U-13, U-15, and U-17 age groups, debuting at Ernakulam.
